title: phlox multiflora subsp. depressa
intro: Phlox multiflora subsp. depressa, also known as Alpine Phlox, is a perennial plant species native to high-altitude regions in North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Phlox multiflora subsp. depressa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations, among others. Alpine Phlox can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat conditions such as colds, sore throats, and wounds.
